data.description:
## Problem
The plan-review code-grounding finders (G1G2/E4/E6/G6) ground assertions ONLY against the origin/main snapshot and never see the ticket's depends_on prerequisites. So a plan that legitimately relies on a symbol/file a prerequisite ticket will create (e.g. `AnalyzerResult` from a blocker) is flagged "missing/unverifiable" and BLOCKED. G1G2 already SAYS "a symbol created by a ticket this ticket depends_on is treated as EXISTING" (`src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_G1G2.md:10`) but that rule is PROSE ONLY — unwired (confirmed: the finder gets only plan_text + rubric; no dependency graph is injected).

## Approach & rationale (G6)
Introduce a machine-parseable inline citation `<symbol> [rebar:<ticket-id>]` and honor it in two layers. Rationale: this is leaner than proactively injecting the recursive dependency graph into every finder (context bloat) and it mirrors the EXISTING `[operator-attested]` inline-tag precedent (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_operator_attested.py`, `_OPERATOR_ATTESTED_TAG_RE`, imported by `det_floor.py:452`). No novel architecture (T1).
- LAYER 1 (deterministic, fail-closed anchor): a new `det_citation.py` (mirroring det_operator_attested) parses `[rebar:<id>]` tokens and verifies each cited id is in the citing ticket's UPSTREAM blocking closure (`ticket depends_on <id>` or `<id> blocks ticket`, recursively). A citation with NO such edge is a deterministic BLOCK — un-gameable, no LLM. det_floor already resolves depends_on/blocks edges among children (`det_floor.py:371-385`); extend to the ticket's own deps.
- LAYER 2 (agent, lazy): the grounding rubrics instruct that an EDGE-VERIFIED `[rebar:<id>]`-cited symbol is treated as EXISTING only after the finder confirms coverage via the already-present `show_ticket` tool (`src/rebar/llm/pai_tools.py` rebar_tools) — pulling the cited ticket's file_impact/plan on demand. Uncited or unbacked assertions still fail closed (unchanged).

## Grounding (E4/G1G2 — verified via Grep/Read)
- CONFIRMED `grep -n "_OPERATOR_ATTESTED_TAG_RE\|ac_item_lines" src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_operator_attested.py` — the inline-tag detector precedent to mirror; imported at `det_floor.py:452`.
- CONFIRMED `det_floor.py:371-385` builds depends_on/blocks edges (intra-child today).
- CONFIRMED `show_ticket` is always in the reviewer tool set (`src/rebar/llm/pai_tools.py` rebar_tools).
- Rubric SoT files exist: `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_{G1G2,E4,E6}.md`. `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md` is GENERATED (regenerate, do not hand-edit).

## Scope
IN: the `[rebar:<id>]` citation grammar + Layer-1 deterministic edge-closure verify (new `det_citation.py`, wired into det_floor blocking checks) + Layer-2 rubric edits (G1G2/E4/E6) + reviewer prompt note for lazy show_ticket coverage + author guidance + regenerate the criteria guide.
OUT (separate tickets): the T8 overlay over-fire (bug 1a05-e3a8); snapshot-pinning for verdict reproducibility; G6/coverage calibration.

## Acceptance criteria (observable + proving command)
- [ ] `det_citation.parse_citations(plan_text)` extracts `[rebar:<id>]` tokens. PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/plan_review/test_det_citation.py::test_parse`.
- [ ] A `[rebar:<id>]` citation whose id is NOT in the ticket's upstream depends_on/blocks closure -> a deterministic floor BLOCK with an actionable message ("add the depends_on/blocks edge or remove the citation"). PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/plan_review/test_det_citation.py::test_unbacked_citation_blocks`.
- [ ] A `[rebar:<id>]` citation WITH a valid upstream edge -> no floor block from this check. PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/plan_review/test_det_citation.py::test_backed_citation_ok`.
- [ ] G1G2/E4/E6 rubrics updated: an edge-verified `[rebar:<id>]`-cited symbol is grounded via a `show_ticket` coverage-check; uncited/unbacked assertions still fail closed. PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/plan_review/test_citation_rubric_contract.py` (asserts the tag + fail-closed clause present in each rubric).
- [ ] Author guidance added. PROVE: `grep -q "\[rebar:" docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md`.
- [ ] Criteria guide regenerated + full plan-review suite green. PROVE: `python -m rebar.llm.plan_review.registry regenerate-criteria-guide && pytest -q tests/unit/plan_review tests/interfaces -k plan_review`.

## Files likely touched
- src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_citation.py (new; mirrors det_operator_attested.py)
- src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_floor.py (wire the new blocking check + expose the ticket's own deps)
- src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_G1G2.md, plan_review_E4.md, plan_review_E6.md
- docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md, docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md (regenerated)
- tests/unit/plan_review/test_det_citation.py (new), tests/unit/plan_review/test_citation_rubric_contract.py (new)

## Estimated scope: M
data.title: Plan-review: honor verified [rebar:<id>] cross-ticket citations so depends_on-created symbols ground instead of false-positive
